reasoning: Auburn's AdjEM (34.7) is 15 points above Creighton's (19.7). Despite Creighton's champion archetype (2014 UConn), the efficiency gap is too wide. Auburn's archetype (2024 Purdue, Runner-Up) confirms a deep-run profile. Auburn's defense (AdjD 93.0) contains Creighton's motion offense.

Near-identical AdjEM (21.8 each) makes this a true toss-up. Texas A&M's archetype (2012 Syracuse, Elite Eight) edges Michigan's (2014 Michigan State, Final Four) in this specific matchup context. Texas A&M's defensive edge (AdjD 94.2 vs 93.9) and SEC physicality decide it.

Iowa State's AdjEM (26.0) is 3.8 points above Ole Miss (22.2). Iowa State's archetype twin (2013 Duke) lost in R64, which is a caution flag, but their defensive profile (AdjD 93.3 vs 97.0) is too strong. Iowa State grinds Ole Miss down.

Michigan State's AdjEM (24.9) holds a 5.5-point edge over Marquette (19.4). MSU's archetype (2010 Duke, Champion) reflects tournament-winning DNA. Despite Marquette's champion twin (2014 UConn), Michigan State's superior defense (AdjD 92.3) and Izzo's tournament pedigree prevail.

Duke's AdjEM (38.5) towers 17.5 points over Baylor (21.0). Duke's archetype (2024 Purdue, Runner-Up) and generational offense (AdjO 130.6) make this a dominant win. Baylor's Final Four archetype (2013 Syracuse) can't overcome this talent gap.

Arizona's 7.3-point AdjEM edge (27.5 vs 20.2) is decisive. Arizona's champion archetype (2016 Villanova, 0.934 similarity) suggests a team built for deep tournament runs. Oregon's champion twin (2014 UConn) is strong but Arizona's offensive firepower (AdjO 124.9) overwhelms in this Pac-12 matchup.

BYU (AdjEM 26.6) slightly edges Wisconsin (26.4) by efficiency alone, but Wisconsin's deliberate tempo (67.9) negates BYU's explosive offense (AdjO 126.8). Wisconsin's archetype (2018 Michigan, Runner-Up) and superior defense (AdjD 96.8 vs 100.2) win this grinding contest.

Alabama's AdjEM (31.1) is 9.9 points above Saint Mary's (21.2). Alabama's pace (75.1 tempo) forces Saint Mary's (61.9 tempo) into an uncomfortable speed. Saint Mary's archetype (2014 Wisconsin, Final Four) thrived at slow pace — Alabama ensures they never get it.

Houston's AdjEM (37.3) vs Gonzaga's (28.1) — a 9.2-point edge. Houston's champion archetype (2019 Virginia) won a title with the same defensive suffocation (AdjD 87.3). Gonzaga's champion twin (2016 Villanova) had elite offense, but Houston's defense is historically elite. Defense wins in March.

Purdue's AdjEM (26.2) edges Clemson's (21.8) by 4.4 points. Purdue's archetype (2024 Purdue, Runner-Up — literally themselves from last year) shows they know how to navigate the tournament. Purdue's offense (AdjO 125.6 vs 117.3) creates a decisive scoring gap.

Close match (AdjEM 24.9 vs 24.0). Kentucky's archetype (2016 Oklahoma, Final Four) and Illinois's twin (2016 Oklahoma, Final Four — same team!) show remarkably similar profiles. Kentucky's higher offensive output (AdjO 123.4 vs 122.5) and roster pedigree tip a coin-flip.

Tennessee's AdjEM (28.3) beats UCLA's (23.6) by 4.7 points. Tennessee's archetype (2015 Wisconsin, Runner-Up, 0.979 — near-perfect match) reached the championship game with this exact defensive profile (AdjD 91.9). UCLA's twin (2012 Syracuse, Elite Eight) can't match Tennessee's defensive intensity.

Florida's AdjEM (33.6) is 14 points above UConn's (19.6). Florida's archetype (2017 Gonzaga, Runner-Up) reached the title game with a similar offensive juggernaut profile (AdjO 127.8). Even UConn's championship pedigree can't overcome this statistical chasm.

Maryland's AdjEM (26.5) is 8.1 points above Colorado State's (18.4). Maryland's archetype (2011 Duke, Sweet 16, 0.986 near-perfect) is a methodical team that grinds out wins. Despite Colorado State's first-round upset momentum, Maryland's defense (AdjD 92.1) suffocates the Rams.

Texas Tech's AdjEM (28.5) holds a 3.8-point edge over Missouri (24.7). Both have strong archetypes — Texas Tech (2024 Purdue, Runner-Up) and Missouri (2016 Oklahoma, Final Four). Texas Tech's balanced profile (AdjO 126.1, AdjD 97.6) wins this clash of high-powered offenses.

St. John's defensive wall (AdjD 90.0 — best in bracket) is the difference despite only a 0.6 AdjEM edge (22.9 vs 22.3). Both archetypes are strong (Final Four twins), but St. John's defense creates the kind of grind that eliminates Kansas's offensive rhythm.
